paper_metadata.title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
paper_metadata.abstract: Scaling language models with more data, compute and parameters has driven significant progress in natural language processing. For example, thanks to scaling, GPT-3 was able to achieve strong results on in-context learning tasks. However, training these large dense models requires significant amounts of computing resources. In this paper, we propose and develop a family of language models named GLaM (Generalist Language Model), which uses a sparsely activated mixture-of-experts architecture to scale the model capacity while also incurring substantially less training cost compared to dense variants. The largest GLaM has 1.2 trillion parameters, which is approximately 7x larger than GPT-3. It consumes only 1/3 of the energy used to train GPT-3 and requires half of the computation flops for inference, while still achieving better overall zero-shot and one-shot performance across 29 NLP tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM introduces a sparsely activated mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture, where each token activates only a subset of the total parameters (96.6B out of 1.2T), enabling efficient scaling of model capacity while reducing inference FLOPs and training energy consumption.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (filtered and unfiltered web pages)
extraction_data.extension: The MoE layer uses a gating function with an auxiliary loss to encourage expert load balancing, ensuring even distribution of tokens across experts during training.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with reduced computational cost and energy consumption
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, few-shot learning, in-context learning, language completion, open-domain question answering, natural language inference
extraction_data.paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM introduces a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture where each Transformer layer alternates between a standard layer and a MoE layer. The MoE layer dynamically activates only two out of 64 experts per token, enabling massive model capacity (1.2T total parameters) while maintaining low computational cost per token (96.6B activated parameters).
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Webpages (143B tokens), Wikipedia (3B), Conversations (174B), Forums (247B), Books (390B), News (650B)
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text generation, few-shot and zero-shot learning
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with high capacity and low inference cost
extraction_data.extension: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with 64 expert layers and 8B total parameters, enabling efficient scaling by activating only a subset of experts per token.

extraction_data.innovation: GLaM introduces a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with sparse activation, where only a subset of experts is activated per token, enabling efficient scaling of language models while maintaining high performance. This approach reduces computational cost during inference compared to dense models of similar size.

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: GLaM introduces a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture where each Transformer layer alternates between a standard layer and a MoE layer. The MoE layer dynamically activates only two out of 64 experts per token, enabling massive model capacity (1.2T total parameters) while maintaining low computational cost per token (96.6B activated parameters).
